uplink.description: TTN example: CBOR indefinite map L+v -> level 1.6155 m, battery 3.32 V
uplink._note: Upstream pls2-l-v6.js decodes this to {Level(m):1.6155023574829102, Battery_Voltage(mv):3320}; we map L->water.level (rounded to 4 dp) and v(mV)->battery(V). Satisfies groundwater (water.level).

uplink.description: Synthetic definite map (A2): L float32 0.5 m, v uint16 3600 mV
uplink._note: Definite-length CBOR map (0xA2). Verified against reference/upstream-codec.js: {Level(m):0.5, Battery_Voltage(mv):3600}.

uplink.description: Synthetic indefinite map: L float32 2.75 m, v uint8 200 mV (low battery)
uplink._note: v encoded as 1-byte uint (0x18 0xC8 = 200 mV). Verified against reference/upstream-codec.js: {Level(m):2.75, Battery_Voltage(mv):200}.

uplink.description: Synthetic level-only map (A1): L float32 0.0 m, no battery
uplink._note: Single-entry map (0xA1) carrying only L. Verified against reference/upstream-codec.js: {Level(m):0}. Confirms groundwater is satisfied by water.level alone.

uplink.description: Error: payload is a bare CBOR integer, not a map

errors: payload is not a CBOR map

uplink._note: 0x05 decodes (in CBOR) to the integer 5; upstream would silently return that as data, but our codec rejects non-map payloads.
